---
title: "Missclasification matrix"
author: "Emilio Otero"
date: "2026-03-16"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
source("stat_func.R")
source("pupulation_model.R")
source("thikanov_inv.R")
# source("simulation_data.R")

library(xtable)
library(glmnet)
library(purrr)
library(patchwork)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(knitr)
library(expm)
library(here)

# read data
# EM ML
iter_only_i <- readRDS("../data/simulations/iter_only_i.rds")
iter_only_u <- readRDS("../data/simulations/iter_no_stop.rds")
iter_only_u <- readRDS("../data/simulations/iter_no_stop.rds")
deconvolved_ml_p_samples_i <- readRDS("../data/simulations/pi_samples_ml_i.rds")
deconvolved_ml_p_samples_u <- readRDS("../data/simulations/pi_samples_ml_u_no_stop.rds")
# Mom 
deconvolved_mom_p_samples_i <- readRDS("../data/simulations/deconvolved_mom_p_samples_i.rds")

# Error
normalized_convolved_sim <- readRDS("../data/simulations/normalized_convolved_sim.rds")

# Oracle 
normalized_truedist_sim <- readRDS("../data/simulations/normalized_truedist_sim.rds")
```


```{r}
# iter_only <- readRDS("iter_only.rds")
# deconvolved_ml_p_samples_i <- readRDS("pi_samples_ml.rds")
```


## Plot stable age distribution

```{r}
# Ensure folder exists
dir.create("figures", showWarnings = FALSE)


# Create plot
p1 <- ggplot(dist_eigenvector, aes(x = age_labels, y = stable_dist)) +  # change 'value' if needed
  geom_col() +
  labs(
    x = "Age class",
    y = "Probability",
    title = "Stable age distribution"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)  # rotate 45 degrees
  )

# Save automatically
ggsave(
  filename = "../figures/age_distribution_p1.pdf",
  plot = p1,
  width = 6,
  height = 4
)

p1
```

## Study error models
```{r}
set.seed(911122)
# Candidates 
absorbing_error_M <- missclass_absorbing(func_sigma = function(x) 3)
absolute_error_M <-  missclass_absolute(func_sigma = function(x) 3)
truncated_error_M <-  truncated_misclassification(func_sigma = function(x) 4)
truncated_error_M_bias <-  truncated_misclassification(func_sigma = function(x) 3, func_mu = function(x) x+4/(x+0.5))



df <- simulate_events(
  rep(5,20),
  truncated_error_M
)

true <- df$true - 0.5
diff <- df$obs - df$true

plot(jitter(true),
     jitter(diff, amount = 0.3),
     xlab = "True bin",
     ylab = "Observed - True",
     xlim = c(0, 30),
     ylim = c(-10, 10))

abline(h = 0, lty = 2)
```

## naiv metod (mätfel)
```{r}


# convolute distribution analytically  with a msicclass matrix
conv_data <- as.vector(t(truncated_error_M)%*%dist_eigenvector$stable_dist)

dist1 <- data.frame(
  age_labels = dist_eigenvector$age_labels,
  stable_dist = dist_eigenvector$stable_dist
) %>%
  mutate(source = "Stable age distribution")

dist2 <- data.frame(
  age_labels = dist_eigenvector$age_labels,  # same x-axis!
  stable_dist = conv_data
) %>%
  mutate(source = "Convoluted distribution")

combined <- bind_rows(dist2, dist1)

p2 <- ggplot(combined, aes(x = age_labels, y = stable_dist, fill = source)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", alpha = 0.5) +  
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "Distribution type",   # 👈 legend title
    values = c(
      "Convoluted distribution" = "Gray40",
      "Stable age distribution" = "Black"
    ) 
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Age class",
    y = "Probability",
    title = "Stable age vs convoluted age distribution"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
    theme(
    legend.position = c(0.8, 0.8),  # x=0.8 (right), y=0.8 (top)
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "black", size = 0.3),
    legend.key.size = unit(0.6, "lines")
  )


# Save automatically
ggsave(
  filename = "../figures/age_distribution_comp_p2.pdf",
  plot = p2,
  width = 6,
  height = 4
)
p2

```

## Numerisk instabilitet plot

```{r}

# Standard deviations from 0.2 to 20
sigma_values <- seq(0.2, 10, length.out = 1000)

  

# Function to calculate condition number for a given sigma
calc_condition <- function(sigma) {
  mat <- truncated_misclassification(
    func_sigma = function(x) sigma,
    func_mu    = function(x) x
  )
  # Compute condition number
  kappa(mat)
}

# Apply function over all sigma values
cond_numbers <- map_dbl(sigma_values, calc_condition)

plot_data <- data.frame(
  sigma = sigma_values,
  variance = sigma_values^2,
  condition_number = cond_numbers
)

thresholds <- round(log(c(1, 5, 100)),3)

p3 <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = sigma, y = condition_number)) +
  geom_point(color = "black", size = 0.5) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = thresholds, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray40") +
  annotate("text", x = c(0.5,2,3.5), y = thresholds, label = paste0("κ=", c(1,5,100)),
           hjust = 0, vjust = -0.3, size = 3.5, color = "gray40") +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(
    x = "Standard Deviation (σ)",
    y = "Logged Condition Number (κ)",
    title = "Logged Condition Number vs standard deviation of error"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()


ggsave(
  filename = "../figures/kondition_number_vs_sd_p3.pdf",
  plot = p3,
  width = 6,
  height = 4
)
p3
```

## True distribution sampling & statistics & table
```{r}
set.seed(19911122)


#  summary statistic of TV from sim_results
tv_result_summary_true <-  compute_tv_all(sim_results_true_dist, stable_dist)

# convert to data frame
df_tv_true <- tv_to_df_summary(tv_result_summary_true)
df_tv_true$model <- "True distribution"
df_tv_true

# Export to ..\tables
table_tex <- kable(df_tv_true, format = "latex", booktabs = TRUE)

writeLines(table_tex, "../tables/df_tv_true_t1.tex")
```


### Convolute samples 
```{r}
# Show convolution 
set.seed(19911122)




# Remove nested list
convolved_sim_cleaned <- lapply(normalized_convolved_sim, function(x) x[[1]])

#  summary statistic of TV from sim_results
tv_result_summary <-  compute_tv_all(convolved_sim_cleaned, stable_dist)

# convert to data frame
df_tv_c <- tv_to_df_summary(tv_result_summary)
df_tv_c


# Export to ..\tables
table_tex <- kable(df_tv_c, format = "latex", booktabs = TRUE)

writeLines(table_tex, "../tables/df_tv_convolution_t2.tex")

```





## Deconvolution ML method informed


```{r}
# Deconvolution steps
set.seed(19911122)

deconvolved_ml_p_samples_i_fixed <- lapply(
  deconvolved_ml_p_samples_i,
  function(x) x[[1]]
)

#  summary statistic of TV from sim_results
tv_result_summary_deconv_i <-  compute_tv_all(deconvolved_ml_p_samples_i_fixed, stable_dist)

# convert to data frame
df_tv_deconv_i <- tv_to_df_summary(tv_result_summary_deconv_i)

df_tv_deconv_i

# Export to ..\tables
table_tex <- kable(df_tv_deconv_i, format = "latex", booktabs = TRUE)

writeLines(table_tex, "../tables/df_tv_deconvolution_i_t3.tex")
```
```{r}
# Deconvolution steps
set.seed(19911122)

deconvolved_ml_p_samples_u_fixed <- lapply(
  deconvolved_ml_p_samples_u,
  function(x) x[[1]]
)

#  summary statistic of TV from sim_results
tv_result_summary_deconv_ml_u <-  compute_tv_all(deconvolved_ml_p_samples_u_fixed, stable_dist)

# convert to data frame
df_tv_deconv_ml_u <- tv_to_df_summary(tv_result_summary_deconv_ml_u)

df_tv_deconv_ml_u

# Export to ..\tables
# table_tex <- kable(df_tv_deconv_i, format = "latex", booktabs = TRUE)
# 
# writeLines(table_tex, "../tables/df_tv_deconvolution_i_t3.tex")
```
# Dconvolution MoM method
```{r}
deconvolved_mom_p_samples_i_fixed_a <- lapply(
  deconvolved_mom_p_samples_i,
  function(x) x[[1]]
)



#  summary statistic of TV from sim_results
deconvolved_mom_p_samples_i_fixed <-  compute_tv_all(deconvolved_mom_p_samples_i_fixed_a, stable_dist)
deconvolved_mom_p_samples_i_fixed <- tv_to_df_summary(deconvolved_mom_p_samples_i_fixed)
deconvolved_mom_p_samples_i_fixed
```



```{r}


df_diff_i <- df_tv_deconv_i %>%
  left_join(df_tv_true, by = "sample_size", suffix = c("", "_true")) %>%
  mutate(
    mean_diff = mean - mean_true,
    median_diff = median - median_true,
    upper_95_diff = upper_95 - upper_95_true,
    method = "ML informed estimator"
  )

df_diff_u <- df_tv_deconv_ml_u %>%
  left_join(df_tv_true, by = "sample_size", suffix = c("", "_true")) %>%
  mutate(
    mean_diff = mean - mean_true,
    median_diff = median - median_true,
    upper_95_diff = upper_95 - upper_95_true,
    method = "ML estimator"
  )



df_diff_MoM <- deconvolved_mom_p_samples_i_fixed %>%
  left_join(df_tv_true, by = "sample_size", suffix = c("", "_true")) %>%
  mutate(
    mean_diff = mean - mean_true,
    median_diff = median - median_true,
    upper_95_diff = upper_95 - upper_95_true,
    method = "MoM estimator"
  )

df_diff_c <- df_tv_c %>%
  left_join(df_tv_true, by = "sample_size", suffix = c("", "_true")) %>%
  mutate(
    mean_diff = mean - mean_true,
    median_diff = median - median_true,
    upper_95_diff = upper_95 - upper_95_true,
    method = "Naive estimator"
  )




df_all <- rbind(df_diff_MoM,df_diff_u,df_diff_c)
df_all$method <- factor(df_all$method,
                        levels = c("Naive estimator", "MoM estimator", "ML estimator"))

```

```{r}
# df_all <- rbind(
#   df_tv_c %>% mutate(method = "Convolved"),
#   df_tv_deconv_i %>% mutate(method = "Deconvolved informed"),
#   df_tv_deconv_u %>% mutate(method = "Deconvolved uninformed")
# )

p8 <- ggplot(df_all, aes(x = factor(sample_size), y = mean_diff, fill = method)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  facet_wrap(~model) +
  labs(
    title = "Excess TV Distance Relative to oracle baseline vs sample size",
    x = "Sample size",
    y = "Excess TV distance relative to oracle baseline",
    fill = "method"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Naive estimator" = "gray",
    "ML estimator" = "blue",
    "MoM estimator" = "orange"
  )) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
p8
ggsave(
  filename = "../figures/barplot_c_MoM_i_TV_minus_baseline_p8.pdf",
  plot = p8,
  width = 8,
  height = 4
)
```
```{r}
df_true_method <- df_all %>%
  mutate(
    mean = mean_true,
    median = median_true,
    upper_95 = upper_95_true,
    method = "Oracle estimator"
  ) %>%
  select(names(df_all))  # keep same structure

df_plot <- bind_rows(df_all, df_true_method)

df_plot$method <- factor(
  df_plot$method,
  levels = c("Naive estimator", "MoM estimator", "ML estimator","Oracle estimator")
)

```


```{r}



p4 <- ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = sample_size, y = mean, group = method, color = method)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  facet_wrap(~ model, nrow = 1) +   # ← two panels side by side
  labs(
    title = "Deconvolution accuracy vs sample size",
    x = "Sample size",
    y = "Mean TV distance",
    color = "Method"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
  "Naive estimator" = "gray",
  "MoM estimator" = "orange",
  "ML estimator" = "blue",
  "Oracle estimator" = "green"
  )) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 0.7)) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)

ggsave(
  filename = "../figures/lineplot_sz_decon.pdf",
  plot = p4,
  width = 8,
  height = 4
)

p4
```


```{r}
get_est_df <- function(vec, method_name, sd_name) {
  data.frame(
    age = seq_along(vec),
    prob = vec,
    method = method_name,
    sd = sd_name
  )
}

all_df <- bind_rows(
  get_est_df(get_median_estimator_fast(convolved_sim_cleaned$sd1[[4]], stable_dist), "Naive", "sd1"),
  get_est_df(get_median_estimator_fast(convolved_sim_cleaned$sd4[[4]], stable_dist), "Naive", "sd4"),
  
  # get_est_df(get_median_estimator_fast(deconvolved_ml_p_samples_i_fixed$sd1[[4]], stable_dist), "ML informed", "sd1"),
  # get_est_df(get_median_estimator_fast(deconvolved_ml_p_samples_i_fixed$sd4[[4]], stable_dist), "ML informed", "sd4"),
  
  get_est_df(get_median_estimator_fast(deconvolved_ml_p_samples_u_fixed$sd1[[4]], stable_dist), "ML", "sd1"),
  get_est_df(get_median_estimator_fast(deconvolved_ml_p_samples_u_fixed$sd4[[4]], stable_dist), "ML", "sd4"),
  
  get_est_df(get_median_estimator_fast(deconvolved_mom_p_samples_i_fixed_a$sd1[[4]], stable_dist), "MoM", "sd1"),
  get_est_df(get_median_estimator_fast(deconvolved_mom_p_samples_i_fixed_a$sd4[[4]], stable_dist), "MoM", "sd4")
)

# ensure age exists
dist_eigenvector$age <- seq_along(dist_eigenvector$stable_dist)

# replicate latent distribution across all panels
dist_all <- do.call(rbind, lapply(unique(all_df$method), function(m) {
  do.call(rbind, lapply(unique(all_df$sd), function(s) {
    data.frame(
      age = dist_eigenvector$age,
      stable_dist = dist_eigenvector$stable_dist,
      method = m,
      sd = s
    )
  }))
}))

# factors for ordering
all_df$method <- factor(all_df$method, levels = c("Naive", "ML", "MoM"))
all_df$sd <- factor(all_df$sd, levels = c("sd1", "sd4"))
dist_all$method <- factor(dist_all$method, levels = levels(all_df$method))
dist_all$sd <- factor(dist_all$sd, levels = levels(all_df$sd))

p_all <- ggplot(all_df, aes(age, prob)) +
  geom_col(fill = "gray60") +
  geom_line(
    data = dist_all,
    aes(age, stable_dist),
    color = "black",
    linewidth = 0.8,
    linetype = "dashed",
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  facet_grid(method ~ sd) +
  theme_minimal()

p_all
# Save
ggsave(
  filename = "../figures/estimate_median_distribution.pdf",
  plot = p_all,
  width = 6,
  height = 4
)

```


```{r}

# wrap your existing function
summarise_mc2 <- function(mat, method_name, sd_name) {
  df <- summarise_mc(mat, method_name)
  df$sd <- sd_name
  df
}

all_df <- bind_rows(
  summarise_mc2(convolved_sim_cleaned$sd1[[4]], "Naive", "sd1"),
  summarise_mc2(convolved_sim_cleaned$sd4[[4]], "Naive", "sd4"),
  
  # summarise_mc2(deconvolved_ml_p_samples_i_fixed$sd1[[4]], "MLinformed", "sd1"),
  # summarise_mc2(deconvolved_ml_p_samples_i_fixed$sd4[[4]], "MLinformed", "sd4"),
  # 
  summarise_mc2(deconvolved_ml_p_samples_u_fixed$sd1[[4]], "ML", "sd1"),
  summarise_mc2(deconvolved_ml_p_samples_u_fixed$sd4[[4]], "ML", "sd4"),
  
  summarise_mc2(deconvolved_mom_p_samples_i_fixed_a$sd1[[4]], "MoM", "sd1"),
  summarise_mc2(deconvolved_mom_p_samples_i_fixed_a$sd4[[4]], "MoM", "sd4")
)

# latent distribution
dist_eigenvector$age <- seq_along(dist_eigenvector$stable_dist)

dist_all <- do.call(rbind, lapply(unique(all_df$method), function(m) {
  do.call(rbind, lapply(unique(all_df$sd), function(s) {
    data.frame(
      age = dist_eigenvector$age,
      stable_dist = dist_eigenvector$stable_dist,
      method = m,
      sd = s
    )
  }))
}))

# ordering
all_df$method <- factor(all_df$method, levels = c("Naive", "ML", "MoM"))
all_df$sd <- factor(all_df$sd, levels = c("sd1", "sd4"))
dist_all$method <- factor(dist_all$method, levels = levels(all_df$method))
dist_all$sd <- factor(dist_all$sd, levels = levels(all_df$sd))

p_all_ci <- ggplot(all_df, aes(age)) +
  geom_ribbon(
    aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper),
    fill = "gray70",
    alpha = 0.4
  ) +
  geom_line(
    aes(y = median),
    color = "gray40",
    linewidth = 0.8
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = dist_all,
    aes(age, stable_dist),
    color = "black",
    linewidth = 0.8,
    linetype = "dashed",
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  facet_grid(method ~ sd) +
  labs(
    x = "Age class",
    y = "Probability",
    title = "Recovered age distributions with Monte Carlo uncertainty"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

p_all_ci

ggsave(
  filename = "../figures/confidence_interval_dist.pdf",
  plot = p_all_ci,
  width = 10,
  height = 6
)
```


```{r}
# 1. Count failures (you already did this)
count_iter_n <- lapply(iter_only_u, function(sd) {
  
  lapply(sd, function(scenario) {
    
    sapply(scenario, function(group_5) {
      
      sum(unlist(group_5) == 1001)
      
    })
    
  })
  
})


# 2. Sample sizes (must match inner list order)
sample_sizes <- c(50, 100, 250, 500, 1000)


sample_sizes <- c(50, 100, 250, 500, 1000)

failure_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(count_iter_n), function(sd_name) {
  
  sd_block <- count_iter_n[[sd_name]]
  
  do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(sample_sizes), function(i) {
    
    # extract all scenarios for this sample size
    vals <- sapply(sd_block, function(scenario) {
      scenario[[i]]
    })
    
    data.frame(
      SD = sd_name,
      SampleSize = sample_sizes[i],
      FailureRate = mean(vals) / 1000
    )
    
  }))
  
}))
failure_df

# Export to ..\tables
table_tex <- kable(failure_df, format = "latex", booktabs = TRUE)

writeLines(table_tex, "../tables/failure_rate_ml.tex")
```

```{r}
extract_tv_df <- function(mat, estimator, sd_label, true_dist) {
  
  tv_vals <- apply(mat, 2, function(p_hat) {
    tv_distance(p_hat, true_dist)
  })
  
  data.frame(
    TV = tv_vals,
    Estimator = estimator,
    SD = sd_label
  )
}

all_tv_df <- bind_rows(
  
  # Naive
  extract_tv_df(convolved_sim_cleaned$sd1[[4]], "Naive", "sd1", stable_dist),
  extract_tv_df(convolved_sim_cleaned$sd4[[4]], "Naive", "sd4", stable_dist),
  
  # ML informed
  # extract_tv_df(deconvolved_ml_p_samples_i_fixed$sd1[[4]], "ML informed", "sd1", stable_dist),
  # extract_tv_df(deconvolved_ml_p_samples_i_fixed$sd4[[4]], "ML informed", "sd4", stable_dist),
  # 
  # ML uninformed
  extract_tv_df(deconvolved_ml_p_samples_u_fixed$sd1[[4]], "ML uninformed", "sd1", stable_dist),
  extract_tv_df(deconvolved_ml_p_samples_u_fixed$sd4[[4]], "ML uninformed", "sd4", stable_dist),
  
  # MoM
  extract_tv_df(deconvolved_mom_p_samples_i_fixed_a$sd1[[4]], "MoM", "sd1", stable_dist),
  extract_tv_df(deconvolved_mom_p_samples_i_fixed_a$sd4[[4]], "MoM", "sd4", stable_dist)
)



violin_1 <- ggplot(all_tv_df, aes(x = Estimator, y = TV, fill = SD)) +
  geom_violin(position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
              alpha = 0.6, trim = FALSE) +
  
  # median only
  stat_summary(
    fun = median,
    geom = "point",
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
    size = 2,
    color = "black"
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "Estimator",
    y = "Total Variation distance",
    fill = "Noise level",
    title = "Distribution of TV distance across estimators (Monte Carlo) | n = 500"
  )
```
```{r}
violins <- violin_2 / violin_1
violins
ggsave(
  filename = "../figures/violin.pdf",
  plot = violins,
  width = 10,
  height = 6
)
```


```{r}
extract_tv_df <- function(mat, estimator, sd_label, true_dist) {
  
  tv_vals <- apply(mat, 2, function(p_hat) {
    tv_distance(p_hat, true_dist)
  })
  
  data.frame(
    TV = tv_vals,
    Estimator = estimator,
    SD = sd_label
  )
}

all_tv_df <- bind_rows(
  
  # Naive
  extract_tv_df(convolved_sim_cleaned$sd1[[2]], "Naive", "sd1", stable_dist),
  extract_tv_df(convolved_sim_cleaned$sd4[[2]], "Naive", "sd4", stable_dist),
  
  # # ML informed
  # extract_tv_df(deconvolved_ml_p_samples_i_fixed$sd1[[2]], "ML informed", "sd1", stable_dist),
  # extract_tv_df(deconvolved_ml_p_samples_i_fixed$sd4[[4]], "ML informed", "sd4", stable_dist),
  # 
  # ML uninformed
  extract_tv_df(deconvolved_ml_p_samples_u_fixed$sd1[[2]], "ML uninformed", "sd1", stable_dist),
  extract_tv_df(deconvolved_ml_p_samples_u_fixed$sd4[[2]], "ML uninformed", "sd4", stable_dist),
  
  # MoM
  extract_tv_df(deconvolved_mom_p_samples_i_fixed_a$sd1[[2]], "MoM", "sd1", stable_dist),
  extract_tv_df(deconvolved_mom_p_samples_i_fixed_a$sd4[[2]], "MoM", "sd4", stable_dist)
)



violin_2 <- ggplot(all_tv_df, aes(x = Estimator, y = TV, fill = SD)) +
  geom_violin(position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
              alpha = 0.6, trim = FALSE) +
  
  # median only
  stat_summary(
    fun = median,
    geom = "point",
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
    size = 2,
    color = "black"
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "Estimator",
    y = "Total Variation distance",
    fill = "Noise level",
    title = "Distribution of TV distance across estimators (Monte Carlo) | n = 100"
  )
```


```{r}
compute_tv_all_per_replica <- function(sim_results, stable_dist) {
  
  lapply(seq_along(sim_results), function(i) {
    
    true_dist <- stable_dist
    
    lapply(sim_results[[i]], function(mat) {
      tv_vec <- apply(mat, 2, function(counts) {
    est_dist <- counts / sum(counts)
    tv_distance(est_dist, true_dist)
  })
    })
    
  })
}
tv_per_rep_c <- compute_tv_all_per_replica(convolved_sim_cleaned,stable_dist)
tv_per_rep_u <- compute_tv_all_per_replica(deconvolved_samples_u,stable_dist)
tv_per_rep_i <- compute_tv_all_per_replica(deconvolved_samples_i,stable_dist)

results_t_test <- data.frame()

for (i in seq_along(tv_per_rep_u)) {
  for (j in seq_along(tv_per_rep_u[[i]])) {

    c_vals <- tv_per_rep_c[[i]][[j]]
    u_vals <- tv_per_rep_u[[i]][[j]]
    i_vals <- tv_per_rep_i[[i]][[j]]

    test_i_u <- t.test(u_vals, i_vals, paired = TRUE)
    test_i_c <- t.test(c_vals, i_vals, paired = TRUE)
    test_u_c <- t.test(c_vals, u_vals, paired = TRUE)

    results <- rbind(results, data.frame(
      model = i,
      sample_size = j,

      mean_c = mean(c_vals),
      mean_u = mean(u_vals),
      mean_i = mean(i_vals),

      diff_i_u = mean(i_vals - u_vals),
      diff_i_c = mean(i_vals - c_vals),
      diff_u_c = mean(u_vals - c_vals),

      p_i_u = test_i_u$p.value,
      p_i_c = test_i_c$p.value,
      p_u_c = test_u_c$p.value
    ))
  }
}
results$model <- factor(results$model,
                     levels = 1:4,
                     labels = c("A", "B", "C", "D"))
  
results$sample_size <- factor(results$sample_size,
                           levels = 1:6,
                           labels = c("10", "25", "50", "100", "150", "200"))
  
# results
```

```{r}
results_long <- bind_rows(
  data.frame(
    model = results$model,
    sample_size = results$sample_size,
    comparison = "I-U",
    diff = round(results$diff_i_u,3),
    p_value = results$p_i_u
  ),
  data.frame(
    model = results$model,
    sample_size = results$sample_size,
    comparison = "I-C",
    diff = round(results$diff_i_c, 3),
    p_value = results$p_i_c
  ),
  data.frame(
    model = results$model,
    sample_size = results$sample_size,
    comparison = "U-C",
    diff = round(results$diff_u_c,3),
    p_value = results$p_u_c
  )
)
results_long <- results_long %>%
  mutate(
    stars = case_when(
      p_value < 0.001 ~ "***",
      p_value < 0.01  ~ "**",
      p_value < 0.05  ~ "*",
      TRUE ~ ""
    )
  )

top10 <- results_long %>%
  select(-p_value) %>%
  arrange(desc(abs(diff))) %>%
  slice(1:10)

# Export to ..\tables
table_tex <- kable(top10, format = "latex", booktabs = TRUE)

writeLines(table_tex, "../tables/diff_tv_top10_t4.tex")
```
